title: Tracking User Feedback Loop
concept: Explicit signals (thumbs up/down) and implicit signals (copying text, edits) are logged to identify failure patterns and build future preference datasets.

exercises.prompt: Why do we track user feedback loops in AI applications?

options.text: To find AI mistakes and make the system smarter over time.
To show ads to the users.
To make the AI run faster on old phones.

exercises.explain: Feedback loops collect data on how the AI performs so developers can fix mistakes and make it smarter.

cards.title: The Silent Teachers
cards.body: How do AI models get smarter over time? They don't just guess! They secretly learn from every single click, copy, and edit you make. Let's explore how we track this feedback loop to build super-smart AI!

cards.title: What is a Feedback Loop?
cards.body: A feedback loop is how we collect information on how our AI is doing. We track explicit signals (actions done on purpose, like clicking a Thumbs Up or Thumbs Down button) and implicit signals (silent clues, like a user copying the text or editing it because it wasn't quite right).

cards.title: The Lemonade Stand Clues
cards.body: Imagine you own a small lemonade stand. If a customer tastes a cup and says 'Yuck, too sour!' that is an explicit signal. If they quietly dump half the cup in the trash or add three packets of sugar on their own, those are implicit signals. Both clues tell you exactly how to make your recipe better next time!

cards.title: Feedback in Action
cards.body: When a user interacts with a chatbot, they might click 'Thumbs Down' (explicit feedback). Then, they manually rewrite the paragraph the AI generated (implicit feedback). The system logs both events to teach the AI what it did wrong and how to write it correctly next time.

cards.title: Myth: Thumbs are Everything!
cards.body: Many people think AI only learns when you click the thumbs-down button. In reality, implicit actions like copying text to your clipboard or immediately rewriting an AI's response are just as important for showing the AI what worked and what didn't.

cards.title: Explicit vs. Implicit Feedback
cards.body: By comparing direct opinions with silent behaviors, developers get a complete picture of how the AI is performing.

right.text: Natural actions like copying text, editing sentences, or deleting answers.

left.text: Direct ratings like Thumbs Up, Thumbs Down, or 5-star feedback.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is an example of an EXPLICIT feedback signal?

options.text: A user copying the text to their clipboard.
A user clicking a 'Thumbs Down' rating button.
A user editing the generated text.
A user closing the window immediately.

exercises.explain: Clicking a thumbs-down button is a direct, intentional rating, which makes it an explicit signal. Copying, editing, and deleting are implicit behaviors.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Explicit signals are the only way to know if an AI answer was helpful.
exercises.explain: Implicit signals are indirect actions like copying or editing, while explicit signals are direct ratings like thumbs up/down. Both are valuable!

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to describe silent, indirect user actions.
exercises.sentence: When a user copies an AI's answer, they are giving an _____ signal that the response was useful.
exercises.explain: Implicit signals are silent, natural actions like copying text. Explicit signals are direct actions like clicking thumbs down.

exercises.prompt: Identify the scenario that shows an implicit feedback signal of a failure pattern.

options.text: AI: 'Here is your recipe.' User: 'Thanks!' (User closes app)
AI: 'The capital is Paris.' User edits the text to say: 'The capital is Rome.'
AI: 'I cannot help with that.' User: 'Okay, no problem.'

exercises.explain: Only Option B represents an AI response being corrected by a human user, which is a classic implicit feedback signal showing that the AI made a mistake.

exercises.prompt: You are building a system to improve a virtual writing assistant. Which strategy will help you find the most failure patterns to build a better dataset?

options.text: Only track thumbs-up ratings to keep things simple.
Ignore users who edit the AI text, as they have already solved their problem.
Log both explicit ratings and implicit actions like text edits to find where the AI fails.
Ask users to write a long feedback email every time they get a bad answer.

exercises.explain: To catch as many AI mistakes as possible, we need both explicit feedback (like direct thumb ratings) and implicit feedback (like watching if users edit the output). Only looking at thumbs-up ratings misses the silent edits!
